sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ['B', 'W', 'c', 'G']:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']))
        if center[0] > 5:
            print(f"Node: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({center[0]:.0f},{center[1]:.0f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Still no change. The magenta block at (44, 42) didn't move when I clicked on it. This is very strange. Let me reconsider the game mechanics entirely.

- etc.
But recently, clicking at (40, 38), (38, 41), (48, 30), and (44, 42) didn't move the magenta block at (44, 42).
Wait, maybe the issue is that the magenta block at (44, 42) is NOT a movable block. Maybe only certain blocks are movable. Let me look at the colors:
- Blue blob: 69 pixels
- Purple block: 9 pixels (this was originally magenta and turned purple when placed)
- Magenta blocks: 4 pixels each (these are the ones I'm trying to move)
Maybe the 4-pixel magenta blocks are not the same type as the blocks I moved earlier. Let me check the early blocks I moved - what color and size were they?
Looking at the history, the blocks I successfully moved were magenta (M) blocks. But maybe the movement mechanic changed, or maybe there's a different condition for movement.
Actually, let me reconsider. Maybe the issue is that I need to click on the TOP-LEFT corner of the block, not the center. The segmentation gives me the center, but the block might be a 2x2 square. Let me check the boundary of the magenta block at (44, 42).
Actually, I realize I've been spending way too many actions without making progress. Let me try a completely different approach: click on the blob itself to see if that triggers level completion.
